* Fix: don't include leading :: in captured alias target name

The template registry stores alias templates without the :: prefix,
so including it in the captured name causes lookup failures during
deferred alias materialization. The class alias path has no
lookup_alias_template guard, so a ::prefixed name passes the
isValid() check but fails at materialization time.

Skip the :: token without appending it to the name builder.

feat(acceleration): CUDA HNSW visited array memory scaling with persistent pool (#4609)

* Initial plan

* feat(acceleration): CUDA HNSW visited array memory scaling with persistent pool

Implements roadmap item #219 (v1.9.0):

- Remove per-invocation cudaMalloc/cudaFree from launchHnswSearchKernel();
  caller now provides pre-allocated d_visited pool (nullptr → overflow signal)
- Add persistent visited bitset pool to CudaHnswTraversalEngine::Impl:
  allocated once in buildIndex() at maxBatchSize × ceil(numNodes/8) bytes,
  freed in freeDevice()
- Implement chunked batch processing in batchSearch(): when pool_capacity <
  num_queries, split into sub-batches of pool_capacity, process serially,
  concatenate on host. Covers both single-pass and multi-pass (large-k) paths.
- Expose CudaHnswTraversalEngine::setMaxBatchSize(size_t n) and
  CUDAVectorBackend::setMaxBatchSize(size_t n)
- Clamp pool to 90% of BackendCapabilities::maxMemoryBytes in buildHnswAnnIndex()
- Set BackendHealthStatus::makeDegraded() via setError() when pool allocation
  fails so getHealthStatus() surfaces the issue to callers
- Add 14 new tests: setMaxBatchSize, hasVisitedPool, chunked correctness,
  degraded health on pool failure
- Update FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md and ROADMAP.md to mark item #219 done

commit.message:
fix: resolve generic TypeScript awaited function calls missing from call graph (#804)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve generic TypeScript function callers missed by impact analysis

When a generic function call is combined with `await` (e.g. `await fn<T>(args)`),
tree-sitter-typescript parses it as a `call_expression` whose `function` field is
an `await_expression` rather than a bare `identifier`. The existing queries only
matched `call_expression { function: identifier }`, so these calls produced no
`@call.name` capture and were silently dropped from the call graph.

Fix: add two new tree-sitter query patterns to `TYPESCRIPT_QUERIES` that handle:
1. `await fn<T>(args)` — awaited generic free call
2. `await obj.fn<T>(args)` — awaited generic member call

Both patterns require the `(type_arguments)` child to be present (which is what
causes tree-sitter to parse the `function` field as an `await_expression`).
Non-generic awaited calls (`await fn(args)`) are unaffected: tree-sitter parses
them as `await_expression { call_expression { identifier } }`, which is still
captured by the existing first pattern.

Also adds a new test fixture `typescript-generic-calls` with two callers of a
generic `verifyToken<T>` function using `await` and three new integration tests.

commit.message:
Initial release of maskproxy

A transparent rewriting reverse proxy in Go that masks an upstream site
from clients by bidirectionally rewriting hostnames and user-defined
string pairs.

Features:
- Target host + all subdomains masked behind proxy address by default
- -exact-domain flag for exact-host-only masking
- Bidirectional string replacement (-replace original:alias,...)
- Case-insensitive replacement (-ci)
- Subdomain routing via /__sd__/ prefix preserving original host
- gzip decompression before body rewrite
- CSP / HSTS / key-pinning headers stripped
- Set-Cookie domain rewrite
- External URL protection (third-party CDN links preserved)
- Body size limit (50 MiB) to prevent OOM
- TLS skip-verify support (-insecure)
